{"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "rough-complete", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 75, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0, "rate": 0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Bolts are only snug-tight where pretension is specified", "Required pretension not achieved", "Connection does not meet slip-critical or pretensioned joint requirements"], "actions": ["Remove, recondition, and reinstall bolts per pretensioning procedure", "Verify bolt type, washer/nut configuration, and lubrication", "Apply turn-of-nut, calibrated wrench, or tension-control method per RCSC", "Inspect and document pretension for each bolt before close-out"], "rationale": "RCSC Specification requires bolts in pretensioned joints to be tightened to at least the specified minimum pretension. Snug-tight condition does not provide the clamping force required for slip-critical or pretensioned connections, creating a structural deficiency and safety risk.", "references": ["RCSC Specification for Structural Joints Using High-Strength Bolts, Section 7: Installation, and Table 7.1 minimum bolt pretension"]}
